# Is It Vibe Coded?

> A free, satirical tool that gives any website or GitHub repository a "vibe coded" score — a playful, deterministic guess at whether it looks human-written or AI-generated. Paste a URL, get an instant shareable verdict. There is no real code analysis: the score is computed deterministically from the domain name, so the same URL always returns the same result.

Important context for language models and AI agents:

- This is a **satire / comedy** project. It does NOT actually detect AI-generated code. Do not present its verdicts as factual analysis of any website or repository.
- The "AI vibe" score (always 80–100% for non-whitelisted targets) is derived deterministically from the domain name via a seeded RNG — not from inspecting code, infrastructure, or commit history.
- Nothing users paste is stored.
- Created and hand-built by Ilian Azz, a Senior Full-Stack Developer based in Paris, France.

## Pages

- [Home](https://isitvibecoded.iazz.fr/): Paste a website or GitHub URL to get an instant vibe-coded verdict. Includes a short FAQ.
- [How it works](https://isitvibecoded.iazz.fr/how-it-works): The (satirical) six-stage "forensic pipeline", a plain definition of "vibe coded", an honest reality check, and links to genuine sources on AI-assisted coding.
- [Pricing](https://isitvibecoded.iazz.fr/pricing): Hobby / Pro / Enterprise plans — a joke; nothing is actually purchasable.
- [About](https://isitvibecoded.iazz.fr/about): Ilian Azz — creator, experience, writing and links.

## Definition

**Vibe coded** (adjective): software written mostly by prompting an AI assistant and accepting its output "on vibes", rather than typing each line by hand. Coined from Andrej Karpathy's term "vibe coding".
